title: 'Save me': Delhi woman's last video call before MP cruise boat tragedy that killed 3
content: A Delhi family's housewarming trip turned tragic in Madhya Pradesh. A cruise boat accident on the Narmada river claimed three lives from the Massey family. The vessel capsized during a sudden storm. Survivors recounted moments of panic and a desperate search for life jackets. Relatives allege negligence by boat operators and authorities.

title: 'Will reward those who faced CAPF brutality': Mamata addresses counting agents
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee expressed confidence in the Trinamool Congress securing over 200 seats, dismissing exit polls as stock market manipulation. She vowed to reward TMC workers who faced alleged brutality from Central Armed Police Forces. Repolling in South 24 Parganas district saw significant voter turnout amidst tight security.

title: BJP leader murder case: Cong Karnataka MLA Vinay Kulkarni disqualified after conviction
content: Karnataka Congress MLA Vinay Kulkarni has been disqualified from the state legislative assembly following his conviction in the murder case of BJP leader Yogeshgouda Goudar. The disqualification, effective from his conviction date of April 15, 2026, will last for six years after his release from life imprisonment.

title: US approves $8.6 bn arms sales to Middle East allies even as Iran war 'terminated'
content: The US has approved over $8.6 billion in arms sales to Israel, Qatar, Kuwait, and the UAE amidst escalating regional tensions with Iran.  Qatar will receive Patriot missile defense replenishment and precision weapon systems. President Trump defended the US military's campaign against Iran's nuclear program, asserting it has weakened Tehran's capabilities and that the US will not rush negotiations.

title: ‘I am vegetarian so need to get my protein’: When Google CEO talked about his breakfast
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai revealed his morning habits. He wakes early, reads news, and enjoys a vegetarian breakfast. Pichai is particular about his tea, describing it as very English. He exercises later in the day. Pichai also shared his favorite Indian dishes from Bengaluru, Delhi, and Mumbai. He enjoys Dosa, Chola Bhatura, and Pav Bhaji respectively.

title: How parking disputes are turning into deadly violence across cities
content: Parking disputes in Delhi and Bengaluru are escalating into violence, with recent incidents involving assaults, firing, and even deaths. These conflicts, often triggered by minor issues like a misplaced vehicle, highlight a growing shortage of space and increasingly confrontational attitudes among residents.

title: ‘I wasn’t desperate’: Agha breaks silence on IND vs PAK handshake snub
content: Salman Ali Agha reignites the Asia Cup 2025 handshake debate, revealing prior knowledge of India's decision to avoid pre-match greetings. He emphasized the responsibility of public figures as role models, highlighting the negative impact on aspiring cricketers. The controversy also saw India decline the trophy from ACC chief Mohsin Naqvi.

title: CEO says ‘When Google chief agrees with Elon, everyone must pay attention’; Musk responds
content: Elon Musk agreed with a post highlighting Google CEO Sundar Pichai's vision of space-based data centers becoming "the new normal" within a decade. This aligns with Musk's own long-held views on utilizing space for large-scale infrastructure. Pichai previously mentioned Google's "moon shot" to place data centers in orbit to harness solar energy, with a first step planned for 2027.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ mints over Rs 1785 cr as ‘Raja Shivaji’ takes lead on Friday
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its steady box office run, grossing over Rs 1785 crore globally after 44 days. Despite facing new competition, including Riteish Deshmukh's 'Raja Shivaji' which took the lead on Friday, 'Dhurandhar 2' added Rs 1.05 crore on its 7th Friday, bringing its India net collection to Rs 1,136.14 crore.

title: Impressed by Claude, Snap CEO says: People will pull away resources from software engineering
content: Snap CEO Evan Spiegel is impressed by AI's rapid advancement, noting tools like Anthropic's Claude are transforming software development. He predicts companies will reallocate resources from engineering to distribution as AI automates coding, with over two-thirds of new code at Snap now written by AI systems.

title: Tom Brady pulled into Vrabel–Russini scandal as Titans report resurfaces
content: Tom Brady has been drawn into the ongoing Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini controversy after a 2020 report resurfaced. The report claimed the Tennessee Titans were not interested in signing Brady, instead backing Ryan Tannehill. Now, amid renewed scrutiny, questions are emerging about whether that narrative reflected reality or served a strategic purpose at the time.

title: Hindu group bids against Christian-Muslim group for same piece of faith land in UK
content: Northstowe's Hindu community faces a significant challenge as they bid for a faith/community land plot to establish their first temple.  Currently, families travel 40 miles for worship, impacting cultural connection.  Hindu Samaj Northstowe proposes an interfaith hub with prayer rooms and a temple, competing with a church-led bid offering multi-faith spaces.

title: India announce Women’s T20 WC squad: Yastika in, Pratika misses out
content: India announced their squad for the 2026 Women’s T20 World Cup in England, with Yastika Bhatia returning after ACL surgery and Pratika Rawal missing out on the T20 setup despite making the Test squad. Harmanpreet Kaur will lead the side, with Smriti Mandhana as vice-captain. Bharti Fulmali, Nandini Sharma and Shree Charani earned places, while Radha Yadav retained her spot. Kashvee Gautam and Amanjot Kaur were unavailable due to injuries.

title: 'No dry outs': Centre assures uninterrupted LPG supply, moves to ease demand with alternative fuels
content: The government assures uninterrupted fuel availability nationwide despite the West Asia crisis impacting global energy markets. Measures are in place to ensure 100% supply of domestic LPG, PNG, and CNG, with household LPG prioritized. Citizens are urged to avoid panic buying and rely on official information.

title: 100% FDI allowed in insurance sector under automatic route, inflows for LIC capped at 20%
content: The central government has announced 100% Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) in insurance companies via the automatic route, enabling full foreign ownership. This move, aligning with the Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha Act, 2025, aims to boost foreign participation, with IRDAI approval required. LIC remains an exception with a 20% FDI limit.

title: Assam election results: Key fights to watch from Jalukbari to Jorhat
content: The high-stakes Assam assembly elections are set for results on May 4, with the BJP aiming for a third consecutive term under Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma. Exit polls suggest an edge for the BJP, while the Congress, led by Gaurav Gogoi, seeks a comeback. Key constituencies like Jalukbari and Jorhat witnessed closely watched contests.

title: India successfully tests long-range hypersonic anti-ship missile off Odisha
content: India has successfully test-fired a long-range hypersonic anti-ship missile off the Odisha coast, reaching a remarkable 1,500 km range. Developed by DRDO, this advanced weapon, capable of speeds up to Mach 10, significantly boosts India's naval defense capabilities. The test validated its precision and ability to engage critical targets, marking a major stride in indigenous defense innovation and self-reliance.

title: 'Manuvadi, anti-women': Rahul rips into BJP over 'pallu' remark on Congress MP
content: Rahul Gandhi has strongly criticized Gujarat BJP chief Jagdish Vishwakarma for an alleged "pallu" remark targeting Congress MP Geniben Thakor. Gandhi accused the BJP of hypocrisy, stating their "Nari Vandana" mask has fallen, revealing an "anti-woman mindset." He warned that women across Gujarat and India would retaliate against such insults.

title: Two-phase census 2027 in UP to begin in May with 5L personnel deployment
content: Uttar Pradesh gears up for Census 2027, a massive two-phase undertaking starting May 2026.  Nearly five lakh personnel will collect housing data and individual demographic details, including caste for the first time since 1931.  A new online self-enumeration option aims to boost efficiency for this crucial policy-planning exercise.

title: 'MS Dhoni can surprise': Ashwin gives major update before CSK vs MI
content: Former India spinner Ravichandran Ashwin believes MS Dhoni is unlikely to return for Chennai Super Kings’ IPL 2026 clash against Mumbai Indians despite recent optimism from CSK batting coach Michael Hussey. Ashwin also feels Rohit Sharma may miss the game due to injury. He added that Chepauk could help Suryakumar Yadav regain form. Ashwin also urged CSK to fix Sarfaraz Khan at No. 3, calling him one of their best batters this season.

title: The Parmigiano vault: How Italy’s ‘cheese banks’ power a 4 billion euro industry
content: In Emilia-Romagna, Italy, hundreds of thousands of wheels of Parmigiano Reggiano cheese are stored in climate-controlled warehouses, serving as valuable collateral for loans. This unique financial system allows producers to overcome cash flow challenges caused by the cheese's lengthy aging process, ensuring the industry's continued operation and growth.

title: Puducherry poll: From CM Rangasamy to ex-CM Vaithilingam, heavyweights to watch out for
content: Puducherry's 2026 Assembly elections are set for a nail-biting finish with a record 89.83% voter turnout. The main battle is between the ruling AINRC-BJP NDA and the Congress-DMK alliance, featuring high-profile leaders in key constituencies. Counting for all 30 seats is scheduled for May 4.

title: BJP-dominant India or...? Decoding layers of saffron supremacy across country
content: The BJP's political dominance across India is undeniable, governing most states and the Centre. However, this influence varies significantly, with strongholds like Uttar Pradesh and Gujarat contrasting with regions where alliances are crucial. The party continues to expand its footprint, but challenges remain in the South and West Bengal, making upcoming elections a key test of its growth trajectory.

title: 'I've been so fearful': Jamieson admits fear of 15-yo Sooryavanshi
content: Delhi Capitals achieved a remarkable seven-wicket victory over Rajasthan Royals, successfully chasing down 226. KL Rahul's composed 75 and Pathum Nissanka's explosive 62 laid the foundation. Pacer Kyle Jamieson, despite an expensive spell, admitted to an unusual fear facing 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, highlighting the importance of early wickets.

title: Assam assembly election results 2026: 10 key constituencies to watch out for
content: Assam recorded over 85% voter turnout in its recent elections, with exit polls projecting a third consecutive term for the BJP led by Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma. The election saw intensified political exchanges between the ruling NDA and a divided opposition, with several key constituencies expected to witness closely fought contests.

title: MP boat tragedy: How labourers risked lives, formed human chain and saved dozens of lives
content: Construction workers near Bargi Dam bravely plunged into choppy waters, risking their lives to rescue over a dozen tourists when a cruise vessel capsized. Without safety gear, these unheralded heroes formed human chains and used ropes, saving many before professional rescuers arrived. Their selfless courage earned them state government recognition and rewards.

title: Venezuela’s crude exports hit highest since 2018 — India, US and other buyers line up
content: Venezuela's crude exports surged 14% to 1.23 million barrels per day in April, reaching their fastest pace in over seven years. This increase, driven by demand from the US, India, and Europe, follows a US agreement that softened sanctions, allowing major trading firms and partners to resume lifting Venezuelan crude.

title: Inside Mumbai Indians' struggles: What's hurting Pandya's men this season?
content: Mumbai Indians are struggling in IPL 2026 with six defeats in eight matches, continuing last season's poor form. Key players like Jasprit Bumrah and Suryakumar Yadav are experiencing slumps, while the absence of Rohit Sharma has disrupted team stability and opening partnerships. The middle order has consistently failed to fire, leading to significant batting collapses.

title: Kerala polls 2026: 20 key constituencies that will shape LDF vs UDF vs NDA battle
content: Kerala's 2026 Assembly elections are shaping into a multi-cornered battle, with 20 key constituencies defining the real contest. These seats, marked by urban change, coastal pressures, and high-profile candidates, will be decisive in determining the state's political direction.

title: US threatens sanctions if shipping firms pay Iran fees for Strait of Hormuz passage
content: The US has warned shipping companies against making payments to Iran for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, citing sanctions risks. This alert comes amid rising tensions and disruptions to global oil trade, with Iran rerouting vessels and charging fees. The US has also imposed a naval blockade, further straining energy markets and complicating diplomatic efforts.

title: The Great 'Gas-by': India bound 45,000-tonne LPG tanker braces for Hormuz crossing
content: An India-linked LPG supertanker, Sarv Shakti, is navigating the Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy route under severe strain due to Middle East tensions. Its successful passage would be a significant breakthrough for India, a major LPG consumer grappling with supply disruptions. This bold move underscores India's determination to secure essential cooking fuel amidst global energy challenges.

title: Days after switch from AAP, two non-bailable FIRs filed against BJP MP Sandeep Pathak in Punjab
content: Two First Information Reports have been lodged against Rajya Sabha MP Sandeep Pathak in Punjab, following his recent switch from the Aam Aadmi Party to the BJP. While official details of the charges remain undisclosed, the registrations occurred shortly after his defection, amidst escalating political realignments in the state. Further information is anticipated.

title: Received alarm-like notification on phone? Here's what it means
content: Mobile users in Delhi-NCR may receive test alert messages. The government is trialing a new disaster alert system across India. This system uses advanced technology to send emergency warnings quickly. Users might get repeated messages in different languages. These are only test notifications, and no action is needed. The system is undergoing evaluation before its official launch.

title: Canada intelligence report says Khalistani extremists pose national security threat
content: Canada's intelligence agency has identified Khalistani elements as a national security threat, citing their promotion of a violent extremist agenda and connections to Canadian citizens. The report highlights the 40th anniversary of the Air India Flight 182 bombing, a deadly attack linked to such groups.

title: 44 peacock deaths in Karnataka’s Tumakuru linked to H5N1 avian influenza
content: Laboratory reports have confirmed that H5N1 avian influenza caused the deaths of 44 peacocks in Tumakuru between April 16 and April 24. Authorities have sounded a high alert in 33 villages within a 10-km radius and are implementing disinfectant spraying and enhanced surveillance to prevent further spread.

title: 'Raja Shivaji' BO day 2: Riteish Deshmukh film crosses Rs 13 crore
content: 'Raja Shivaji', directed by and starring Riteish Deshmukh as Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, has crossed Rs. 12.21 crore India net on Day 2 of its theatrical run, driven by extraordinary audience response in the Marathi-speaking belt and the viral impact of Salman Khan's surprise cameo as loyal warrior Jiva Mahala. Made under a budget of approximately Rs. 100 crore, the film features Sanjay Dutt as Afzal Khan, Vidya Balan as Badi Begum, Abhishek Bachchan as Sambhaji Shahaji Maharaj, Bhagyashree as Jijabai, Fardeen Khan as Shah Jahan, Amole Gupte as Mohammed Adil Shah and Genelia Deshmukh as Saibai, with music by Ajay-Atul and cinematography by Santosh Sivan.

title: Oil marketing companies seek LPG, petrol, diesel price hike as Iran war deepens losses
content: Middle East conflict escalates, pushing crude oil prices above $126 per barrel and straining India's state-run oil companies. Facing mounting losses, these companies seek permission to raise pump prices, but the government hesitates, balancing consumer protection against financial pressures and potential inflation.

title: Dining out set to get costlier in Kerala as LPG prices surge; hotels call May 6 shutdown
content: With the latest hike, the price of a commercial cooking gas cylinder has crossed ₹3,000 in cities such as Kochi, where it reportedly fetches over ₹3,400 in the black market

title: Assembly elections 2026 LIVE: Exit polls manipulated by BJP to mislead voters, says Gaurav Gogoi
content: Repolling in 15 booths across two constituencies in West Bengal was largely incident-free under heavy security deployment, even as protests were reported from Falta where a decision on fresh polling is still pending

title: CM directs officials to fill 24,300 vacancies cleared by Finance Department, address drinking water issue
content: Siddaramaiah emphasises that it is the duty of officials to implement Budget announcements;  points out that 15 announcements from last year’s Budget are yet to be implemented

title: Kerala Assembly elections: All set for counting in Pathanamthitta
content: The strongrooms will be opened at 7 a.m. on May 4 in the presence of candidates or their authorised agents. Counting will begin at 8 a.m.

title: Annamayya Collector urges swift action on SC/ST Atrocities cases
content: Nishant Kumar underlines the need for close coordination among Police, Revenue, and line departments involved in the enforcement process during a District Vigilance and Monitoring Committee review meeting

title: T.N. election: Vijay to emerge as decisive leader; 2026 verdict will be historic, says Sengottaiyan
content: Addressing reporters at the party office in Karattur near Gobichettipalayam, he described the TVK as one rooted in “sacrifice and humanism” and committed to grassroots work

title: Telangana government to clear ₹6,000 crore retirement benefits in 100 days, promises Revanth Reddy
content: A committee of officials has been directed to immediately submit its report on the Pay Revision Commission, says Chief Minister, adding decision on PRC implementation would be taken based on the committee’s recommendations

title: Passing out parade of 60 Kerala Police Sub-Inspector trainees held
content: State Police chief Ravada Chandrasekhar reviews parade at Kerala Police Academy; top performers honoured

title: Prasoon Joshi appointed chairman of Prasar Bharati
content: Before this appointment, the lyricist has served as chairperson, Central Board of Film Certification (CBFC), Mumbai since August 2017

title: India at Venice Biennale 2026 curtain raiser: Many voices, one resonance
content: From soil and thread to immersive myth and monumental sculpture, India’s Venice Biennale 2026 presence expands beyond the pavilion into a layered meditation on home and identity

title: Treasure hunter held with explosives in Sathya Sai district
content: Police seize drilling machines, batteries, chargers, 25 gelatin sticks, drilling bits, and some other equipment related to excavation activities from the accused

title: APTransco taps IIT talent for real-time grid challenges
content: Summer Internship Programme - 2026 brings students into live operations, focuses on AI, forecasting and automation with stipends up to ₹25,000

title: Access to generic Risdiplam sought under policy for rare diseases for people with Spinal Muscular Atrophy
content: The recent availability of India-manufactured generic Risdiplam offers a practical pathway, patients write in an appeal to the PM; access to treatment is time sensitive, with delays having the potential to cause irreversible deterioration in the life-limiting neuromuscular condition

title: Kalaburagi joins global ‘City Nature Challenge’ despite intense heatwave
content: City Nature Challenge is an international community science initiative focused on documenting urban biodiversity

title: LPG tanker linked to India, Sarv Shakti, crosses Strait of Hormuz
content: The Marshall Islands-flagged VLGC, with an Indian crew aboard, is carrying around 45,000 tonnes of LPG; while sailing, it broadcasted ‘India/Indian crew’ on the automatic identification system

title: Kerala Cabinet decides to provide financial assistance to victims of Valparai accident
content: An amount of ₹10 lakh each will be given to the families of five of the deceased who did not have insurance cover. Other deceased teachers were covered under insurance

title: Enumerators begin housing census in Andhra Pradesh
content: Enumerators have been deployed across urban and rural areas, using a digital application to collect responses to over 30 questions

title: NGT order warns of fines to southern States if clean air funds are not utilised
content: The tribunal has ordered all six southern States and Puducherry to ensure time-bound implementation of their State Action Plans; these directions assume significance as it puts the spotlight on air pollution governance in the southern States — an issue that has largely been dominated by the northern regions, particularly Delhi-NCR

title: Chittoor MLA ‘withdraws’ decision to surrender security cover amid assault controversy
content: The issue relates to an incident in which a Dalit woman working with the MEPMA wing of Chittoor Municipal Corporation was allegedly assaulted by a group said to be linked to the MLA

title: Census operations director in U.P. says exercise to include caste enumeration
content: In the context of population, if you are ordinarily residing in a particular location, you are considered part of that area's population and will be enumerated, says the director of census operations in the State, Sheetal Verma

title: No cause for panic about heat conditions: Union Minister Jitendra Singh
content: The Minister highlighted that the government is fully prepared to address heat-related challenges and stressed the need for coordinated efforts at the State and district levels to ensure access to drinking water, functioning cooling arrangements, and an uninterrupted power supply.

title: "India's Best Defence Against Global Risks Is To Electrify Everything," Says Sagar Adani
content: Adani said India's long-term constraints - water security, food systems, and digital growth - all run into the same bottleneck: reliable, affordable energy.

title: "We're Like Pirates": Trump On US Seizing Iranian Oil From Ships In Hormuz
content: "We ... land on top of it and we took over the ship. We took over the cargo, took over the oil. It's a very profitable business," Trump said on Saturday while addressing a rally in Florida.

title: 2 Women Killed, 15 injured As Bus Carrying Pilgrims Overturns In Gujarat
content: Two women were killed and at least 15 passengers were injured as a bus carrying pilgrims overturned when a Nilgai suddenly crossed the road in Junagadh district on Saturday, police said.

title: Boy Killed In Agra For Plucking Watermelons From Field Owned By Family: Cops
content: Two persons have been arrested after a 13-year-old boy was killed in Agra for allegedly plucking watermelons from a field owned by his family, police said on Saturday, adding that the incident has led to tension in the area.

title: Mali Launches Probe Into Soldiers Over Role In Al-Qaeda-Linked Attacks
content: The list of potential accomplices includes 3 active duty soldiers, a retired soldier and a soldier who had been "dismissed" and was killed in fighting

title: Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Voting Live Updates: 86% Turnout In Magrahat Paschim, 88% In Diamond Harbour Till 5 PM
content: Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Voting Live Updates: The repolls were ordered in 11 polling stations of the Magrahat Paschim assembly constituency and four in Diamond Harbour.

title: "Time Will Tell Whether This Will Disrupt Or Stabilise World Energy Markets": Sagar Adani On Gulf Shifts
content: Adani warned that the broader conflict in West Asia is "not good for the world," describing it as a "lose-lose" situation for all involved.

title: "Central Staff, Give Credence": Top Court To Trinamool Over Plea Against Poll Body
content: "They're Government Staff, Give Them Some Credence": Supreme Court To Trinamool Over Petition Against Election Commission

title: India's Latest Nuclear Reactor Win Can Lead To 700 Years Of Energy Security
content: India has achieved one of the most consequential milestones in its atomic energy journey, as the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) achieved criticality.

title: Analysis: Is Kerala Reverting To Its Old Pattern Of Alternating Governments?
content: . The UDF is likely to win not just because of its positioning but because the Left has exhausted its current cycle

title: Iran Claims Trump Rejected New Proposal That Would Open Strait Of Hormuz
content: Under the proposal, the war would end with a guarantee that Israel and the United States would not attack again, an Iranian official reportedly said.

title: "Save Me": Delhi Woman's Last Call Before She Died In Jabalpur Boat Tragedy
content: What began as a family trip to attend a housewarming ceremony in Madhya Pradesh turned into a devastating tragedy for a Delhi family after three of its six members died when a cruise boat capsized in the Bargi reservoir on the Narmada river, relative

title: Probe Committee Finds Lapses In Ghee Procurement For Tirupati Laddu
content: According to the report, the problem was not just a failure to detect adulteration but a result of how procurement rules were framed and implemented.

title: 31 Bases, 68,000 Personnel: How US Is Stacked Up Militarily In Europe
content: The United States is withdrawing 5,000 troops from NATO ally Germany, following a spat over Iran war between Trump and Chancellor Friedrich Merz.

title: Tight Security Around EVM Strong Rooms In Assam: Chief Electoral Officer
content: Assam Chief Electoral Officer Anurag Goel underlined the strict security measures in place at the strong rooms, saying that central observers and security personnel are carrying out their duties diligently

title: New Phone Alert System Aimed At Protecting Lives During Disasters: Jyotiraditya Scindia
content: The minister said the system does not depend on mobile data or user databases and works across all telecom operators

title: "Can't Eat, Sleep": Jabalpur Boat Pilot To NDTV On Tragedy That Killed 9
content: Boat pilot Mahesh Patel said the dismissal will also affect his family, which had been surviving solely on the low salary he earned from the job.

title: From Welfare To Fraud: How A Coaching Scheme Went Off Track
content: The Jai Bhim Mukhyamantri Pratibha Vikas Yojana was launched in 2017 with just 8 empanelled coaching institutes, helping around 5,000 students.

title: Feeling hot-headed this summer? You’re not alone. Science explains why
content: Priyanshi Rastogi is a journalist with The Times of India. She primarily covers national and international news, along with business affairs. Her work focuses on the latest political events, in-depth explainers, features, offbeat articles and viral social media trending stories. Read More

title: Trumpiana: Potboiler on a platter!
content: a self-styled 'friendly federal assassin' interrupts journalists' dinner with wannabe king Donald Trump . the plot was served with spring pea and burrata salad and vintage wine . 147 of 188 bottles of wine costing $76 apiece vanished in jiffy .
